dossier.summary: Extensive open-source research returned no publicly indexed social media profiles, professional listings, or other web presence directly attributable to a 'Mateo Amado Ares' residing in Oleiros, A Coruña, Spain. The only publicly documented individual sharing the 'Amado Ares' double surname in the A Coruña metropolitan area is 'Jose Antonio Amado Ares', listed as Director de Compras at steelmaker Megasa and based in A Coruña — a possible family relation. No confirmed employer, role, or lifestyle signals were found for the subject. Email found in 4 breach(es); registered on: trello.com, wattpad.com, duelingnetwork.com, edmodo.com | 1 risk flag(s) raised (plaintext passwords, sensitive data, or pastes) | Email found in 4 breach(es); earliest 2017, latest 2024

llm_summary.summary: Mateo Amado Ares is the named debtor, residing at Oleiros, A Coruña, Galicia, Spain, though this address has not been independently confirmed via public records. No employer, occupation, or social media presence has been identified for the subject. The email address mateoamadoares@gmail.com appears in 4 data breaches spanning 2017 to 2024, with accounts registered on Trello, Wattpad, DuelingNetwork, and Edmodo. One exposed password from the DuelingNetwork breach was identified as a weak hash. A separate individual, Jose Antonio Amado Ares, is publicly listed as Director de Compras at Megasa (steel company) in A Coruña, but no confirmed family relationship to the subject exists. The outstanding debt is €2,077.00 from a personal loan, 12 months old, originated in Spain. One call attempt has been made with no answer (rings out). A legal asset check returned assets not seizable.

breaches_details.details: The Trello Data Scraping Incident in January 2024 resulted in over 15 million email addresses, names, and usernames being posted for sale on a popular hacking forum. This data was collected by enumerating a publicly accessible resource using email addresses sourced from previous breach datasets. Trello has stated that this incident did not involve unauthorized access to their systems.

Wattpad, a self-publishing platform that claims to be “the world’s most-loved social storytelling platform,” suffered a data breach exposing all of its users. The leaked database included more than 270 million records with more than 268 million unique email address and password combinations. Exposed data includes email address, date of birth, gender if provided, IP address upon sign up, if signed up before 2017, display name, account name, and salted and cryptographically hashed passwords.
breaches_details.xposed_data: Names;Usernames;Dates of birth;Passwords;IP addresses;Genders;Social media profiles;Geographic locations;Passwords

breaches_details.details: Online fan game site Dueling Network, a now-defunct Flash game based on the Yu-Gi-Oh trading card game got breached and exposed of its 6.5 million users email as well as poorly hashed passwords.

Edmodo, an education technology company providing a learning management system, announced that it had suffered a data breach. User names, email addresses, and hashed passwords were acquired by an unauthorized third party. The passwords were “hashed” (or encrypted) using the strong and robust bcrypt algorithm. In 2018, the data breach was reported by the company.
